channel.title: sequencer on SoundsBrown
channel.description: Recent content in sequencer on SoundsBrown
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Baby10 Sequencer
item.description: Classic “Baby 10” sequencer based on 4017 decade counter IC. Uses external clock signal for timing. Each step has a pot for setting the output voltage (0-5V), a gate output and an indicator LED. Global CV jack outputs the sequenced voltages. Buttons allow manual stepping and resetting to step 0. https://hackaday.com/2016/01/14/oh-baby-baby10-build-a-classic-analog-music-sequencer/ Very laborious build due to large number of front panel components, would not make again. The muxer sequencer is at least as useful in hindsight and was much easier to build.

item.title: Muxer Sequencer
item.description: This is a sequencer-ish module built around a 4051 multiplexer/demultiplexer IC. The IC is basically an 8-way selector switch which connects the COM jack to one of the 8 in/out jacks. The selection is done by setting 3 address bits. The IC works in both directions, so you can send 1 input to 8 alternating outputs, or take 1 output from 8 alternating inputs. The module is driven by an Arduino, which consumes the clock input and manages the address lines (each clock pulse advances the sequence to the next step).
